test_name: September 2025 International Form B SAT English Module 2
passage:
As used in the text, what does the word “define” most nearly mean?
The following text is adapted from the 1895 poem “Shadow River” by Emily Pauline Johnson (Tekahionwake). The speaker is looking at the reflections of plants in a river. The little fern-leaf, bending
Upon the brink, its green reflection greets,
And kisses soft the shadow that it meets
With touch so fine,
The border line
The keenest vision can’t define;
So perfect is the blending.
question_text: As used in the text, what does the word “define” most nearly mean?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Perceive

choices.label: B
choices.text: Abbreviate

choices.label: C
choices.text: Restate

choices.label: D
choices.text: Explain

Which choice best describes the overall structure of the text?
High-speed rail systems, in which trains can move at great speeds, are expanding in many countries because high-speed rail can reduce the number of automobiles on the road and ultimately conserve energy. In Denmark, for instance, 56 kilometers of high-speed rail lines are in operation as of 2023, and 717 kilometers are under construction.
question_text: Which choice best describes the overall structure of the text?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The text rejects a long-held belief and then proposes a different interpretation.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The text questions a course of action and then suggests alternatives to that action.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The text summarizes an argument and then offers a challenge to that argument.

choices.label: D

test_name: September 2025 International Form B SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole?
In 1965, poet and scholar Dudley Randall founded Broadside Press in Detroit, Michigan. With the goal of making the work of Black poets widely available to the public, Randall published and distributed broadsides: individual poems printed on single sheets of paper. <u>Since these broadsides became associated with the innovative aesthetics of the Black Arts Movement (1965–1975)</u>, Randall’s publication format may likewise seem to have been pioneering, but, in fact, broadside poetry had also flourished in seventeenth-century London, where single-sheet popular ballads flooded the marketplace.
question_text: Which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It undermines the argument that Randall’s method for publishing poetry was a largely novel one.

choices.label: B
choices.text: It identifies a reason for a possible misconception regarding Randall’s publication of broadsides.

choices.label: C
choices.text: It explains why Randall’s broadsides were important to the Black Arts Movement.

choices.label: D
choices.text: It provides context for Randall’s decision to publish work by Black poets as broadsides.

test_name: September 2025 International Form B SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which choice best states the main idea of the text?
The following text is adapted from Daniel Defoe’s 1704 nonfiction book *The Storm*.
If I judge right, ’tis the duty of an historian to set everything in its own light, and to convey matter of fact upon its legitimate authority, and no other: I mean thus, (for I would be as explicit as I can) that where a story is vouched to him with sufficient authority, he ought to give the world the special testimonial of its proper voucher, or else he is not just to the story: and where it comes without such sufficient authority, he ought to say so; otherwise he is not just to himself.
question_text: Which choice best states the main idea of the text?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Because no memories can be trusted, all historians are forced to admit that some records of events may be, in part, false.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The only figures that a historian should quote are those who are widely viewed as credible.

choices.label: C
choices.text: It is difficult for historians to be completely accurate, and eventually they will feel remorse for some of their work.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Historians should clearly indicate the extent to which each of their sources is trustworthy.

test_name: September 2025 International Form B SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which choice best states the main idea of the text?
In their meta-analysis of research on advergames (video games developed to promote products or services), Zeph M.C. van Berlo et al. confirm that such games, though they can elicit player interest, may not facilitate subsequent recall of product and brand information. This phenomenon can be explained by the finite nature of cognitive capacity as it is articulated in Annie Lang’s limited capacity model of motivated mediated message processing. In this case, players’ cognitive resources are directed foremost toward the advergame’s mechanics, leaving little or no capacity for encoding and storing the information the advertiser intends to be salient.
question_text: Which choice best states the main idea of the text?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The limited capacity model of motivated mediated message processing developed by Lang provides a means of explaining the finding by van Berlo et al. that players may not readily recollect the brand and product information embedded in advergames.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The meta-analysis by van Berlo et al. reveals that higher engagement in advergame mechanics is linked to lower effectiveness in persuading players to purchase particular brands and products.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Research by van Berlo et al. corroborates Lang’s conclusion that because people predominantly focus on game mechanics when playing video games, it is difficult for advergames to communicate brand and product information in ways that are highly memorable.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Although the limited capacity model of motivated mediated message processing developed by Lang suggests otherwise, advergames can succeed as marketing tools, provided that they achieve a balance between game mechanics and the promotion of a brand or product.

test_name: September 2025 International Form B SAT English Module 2
passage:
Based on the texts, the author of Text 1 and the author of Text 2 would most likely agree on which point?
Text 1
For thousands of years, O’odham farmers in the Sonoran desert of the southwestern US and northern Mexico have cultivated organ pipe fruit and beans, sometimes planting these species together so that the organ pipe cacti provide shade for tepary beans. Doing so helps protect tepary beans from the harshest heat and light and thereby helps prevent soil moisture from evaporating.
Text 2
Tepary beans are well adapted to growing in the desert but grow best when shaded. Inspired by O’odham farmers, who often strategically plant tepary beans in the shade of sun-hardy species like organ pipe cacti for protection from the sun and heat, Gary Nabhan and colleagues planted tepary beans in the shade of solar panels in the Sonoran desert and found that the plants grew well, suggesting to Nabhan and colleagues that the panels provide a benefit similar to that provided by organ pipe cacti.
question_text: Based on the texts, the author of Text 1 and the author of Text 2 would most likely agree on which point?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Organ pipe cacti can provide shade that protects tepary beans from high-intensity heat and light.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Compared with Nabhan’s approach, the O’odham approach has the advantage of producing beans.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Organ pipe cacti grow best when planted in shaded areas, while tepary beans do not require shade to thrive.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Nabhan’s team’s method could be refined to more actively prevent soil moisture from evaporating.

test_name: September 2025 International Form B SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which choice best describes data from the table that weaken the students’ claim?
While observing birds for a biology class, a group of students noticed a Western sandpiper (*Calidris mauri*), a bird of the order Charadriiformes (shorebirds), slowly move away, make loud noises, and feign an injured wing as the group approached the bird’s ground nest. Researching this behavior, the students learned that this phenomenon is commonly referred to as “broken-wing display” and is meant to lure predators away from the nesting site and toward a seemingly vulnerable adult bird. Upon learning of other birds that also exhibit the behavior they had observed, the students hypothesized that the phenomenon is exclusive to shorebirds.
question_text: Which choice best describes data from the table that weaken the students’ claim?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Unlike the common sandpiper, the South American snipe is not known to perform broken-wing display, even though both are in the order Charadriiformes.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Neither the West Peruvian dove nor the South American snipe is known to perform broken-wing display.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The common sandpiper, in the order Charadriiformes, is known to perform broken-wing display, and the West Peruvian dove, in the order Columbiformes, is not.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Both the yellow-billed cuckoo and the sandhill crane are known to perform broken-wing display.

After sample A0106 was retrieved from the asteroid Ryugu, a portion of it was subjected to an extraction protocol in which small amounts of pulverized material were rinsed, sequentially, with hexane, dichloromethane, methanol, and finally a mixture of dichloromethane and methanol. Each rinse created a separate extract suitable for spectral analysis. Based on the table, both alkane and dimethyl sulfide compounds must have been present in the pulverized sample before the methanol-only extraction, because ______

choices.label: B

test_name: September 2025 International Form B SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which statement, if true, would most strongly support Wade’s argument?
In medieval England, singers and minstrels made livings as traveling performers, but the pieces they performed have been a mystery, thought to be lost or never committed to paper at all. Recently, however, James Wade has argued that a manuscript collection compiled in the fifteenth century by Richard Heege contains three pieces likely copied from a repertoire book belonging to a minstrel working in the area around Heege’s home. Wade cites features of the texts indicative of live performance, such as directly appealing to the audience, extensive use of repetition (allowing for easy memorization), and requests for tips (payment) from listeners.
question_text: Which statement, if true, would most strongly support Wade’s argument?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Heege’s collection contains the earliest examples of the three pieces in question, but each of those pieces occurs in other manuscript collections compiled after Heege’s.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Traveling minstrels are thought to have performed mainly for elite audiences, and other parts of Heege’s collection reflect his interest in works with popular appeal.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Features like repetition also occur in other texts from the period that were widely read but are not known to have been performed for live audiences.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The three pieces in question contain references that presume that the audience is familiar with Radford and other small villages near where Heege lived.

test_name: September 2025 International Form B SAT English Module 2
passage:
Based on the text, which finding, if true, would best account for the discrepancy Etta-Nkwelle observed?
Economists have demonstrated that herding—in which individuals converge on the same action by imitating the economic decisions of one or more peers—occurs among investors. Investigating whether herding also occurs among nations in matters of economic policy, Maru Etta-Nkwelle examined changes in the foreign currency reserves of emerging economies in various world regions in the years following the widespread financial crisis of 1997. Etta-Nkwelle posited that herding would manifest in regional peer countries converging on the same foreign currency accumulation rate regardless of each country’s observable circumstances. She found that Asian countries showed evidence of herding but African countries did not.
question_text: Based on the text, which finding, if true, would best account for the discrepancy Etta-Nkwelle observed?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The majority of the currency held in reserves by the African countries was foreign currency, whereas the majority of the currency held in reserves by the Asian countries was domestic currency.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The African countries tended to accumulate a variety of foreign currencies for their reserves, whereas the Asian countries tended to favor a single foreign currency for their reserves.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Most of the African countries belonged to international economic unions that set individual foreign-currency accumulation policies for each member, whereas the Asian countries were largely free to set their own policies.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The 1997 financial crisis resulted in immediate declines in many of the African countries’ foreign currency reserves, whereas the crisis had few short-term effects on the foreign currency reserves of most of the Asian countries.

test_name: September 2025 International Form B SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which choice most logically completes the text?
Introducing a new species into a delicate ecosystem can often be harmful. The hwamei and other fruit-eating birds have established themselves on the Hawaiian island of Oahu after being brought there in the last 150 years. Many plant species on the island rely on the activity of fruit-eating birds to maintain and increase their populations. In recent studies, hwameis have been found to spread seeds of native forest plants that are at risk of extinction, such as the kawau tree and the alahee tree, suggesting that ______
question_text: Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.label: A
choices.text: non-native birds have had both positive and negative effects on Oahu’s native plants.

choices.label: B
choices.text: although non-native birds initially damaged the plants in Oahu’s ecosystem, they are now proving useful to them.

choices.label: C
choices.text: rather than harming Oahu’s ecosystem, some non-native birds provide an important benefit on Oahu.

choices.label: D
choices.text: non-native birds, such as the hwamei, are unlikely to eat fruits from non-native forest plants on Oahu.

test_name: September 2025 International Form B SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which choice most logically completes the text?
Lichens lack root systems, so they must absorb water directly from the air, dew, and even tree bark and retain it in their bodies. A team of scientists recently used computer simulations to evaluate the importance of water stored in tree bark (bark water) for lichens in the Mediterranean region. In the control simulation, lichens had access to all water sources; in the experimental simulation, lichens had access to all sources except bark water. Compared to the control, the experimental simulation predicted a decrease in the lichens’ overall biomass production of 21%. The experimental simulation (but not the control simulation) also predicted a decrease in the survival of certain species of lichen during the summer due to evaporative water loss, suggesting that ______
question_text: Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.label: A
choices.text: only some specialized lichen species are able to absorb bark water during long periods of high evaporation.

choices.label: B
choices.text: access to bark water may help offset the effects of seasonal water loss for some lichen species.

choices.label: C
choices.text: although lichens can absorb water from multiple sources, most of the water they absorb is bark water.

choices.label: D
choices.text: bark water absorption reduces biomass production in lichens but makes them more resistant to seasonal changes in conditions.

• Artist Douglas Coupland’s metal sculpture Digital Orca is on display at a public convention center in Vancouver, Canada.

passage:
The student wants to make a generalization about free-elections clauses in state constitutions. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• US states are the primary regulators of federal and state elections and bear much of the responsibility to establish voting rights.
• All fifty state constitutions contain clauses that confer the right to vote on eligible citizens.
• Thirty state constitutions also include some form of a free-elections clause.
• These clauses more broadly mandate that elections be conducted in a fair manner.
• Further guidance with regard to voting rights and conducting elections is legislated by state lawmakers.
question_text: The student wants to make a generalization about free-elections clauses in state constitutions. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Although all states’ constitutions have clauses conferring the right to vote, free and fair elections aren’t mandated with the same consistency.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Every state constitution has some form of a free-elections clause, by which a state confers voting rights on eligible citizens.

choices.label: C
choices.text: States bear most of the responsibility for establishing voting rights and conducting fair elections.

choices.label: D
choices.text: In addition to constitutional provisions such as free-elections clauses, state lawmakers provide election guidance by means of legislation.

test_name: September 2025 International Form B SAT English Module 2
passage:
The student wants to emphasize a historical similarity between Hadid’s and Gehry’s respective projects. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• The 1988 exhibition Deconstructivist Architecture at the Museum of Modern Art (MoMA) in New York City featured projects by seven architects.
• The exhibition was influential in popularizing deconstructivist design principles in the late twentieth century.
• It included architectural plans for The Peak, a 1983 project by Iraqi British architect Zaha Hadid featuring jagged geometric forms jutting out from an artificial cliff.
• It included a model of Gehry House, a 1978–88 project by US architect Frank Gehry featuring jagged geometric forms extending from a Dutch colonial exterior.
• Curators Philip Johnson and Mark Wigley describe the exhibition’s projects as subverting conventional assumptions “about order, harmony, stability, and unity.”
question_text: The student wants to emphasize a historical similarity between Hadid’s and Gehry’s respective projects. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The popularization of deconstructivist design in the late twentieth century owes much to the architects featured in MoMA’s 1988 exhibition, Hadid and Gehry among them.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Hadid’s and Gehry’s respective projects date to 1978, a decade before MoMA’s influential 1988 exhibition Deconstructivist Architecture.

choices.label: C
choices.text: With their jagged geometric forms, The Peak and Gehry House both subvert conventional assumptions “about order, harmony, stability, and unity” (Johnson and Wigley).

choices.label: D
choices.text: The Peak and Gehry House were featured in an influential 1988 MoMA exhibition that popularized deconstructivist design principles in the late twentieth century.
